assertions.text: Another car pulled out in front of the reporting car on a fast road.
The reporting car was traveling around 110 km/h before braking.
The reporting car performed an emergency brake maneuver.
There was a single impact (the other vehicle clipped the reporting car once).
The reporting car was clipped by the other car (the other car struck the reporting car while pulling out in front).
The emergency braking occurred before the impact.
After the impact, the reporting car slowed to walking pace.
The collision was a light/one-time clip (minor contact).

verdicts.evidence: The narrative claims another vehicle pulled out in front; telemetry does not record other-vehicle positions or turn/pull-out events. GPS speed and accelerometer show motions but cannot identify another vehicle; no channel provides other vehicle presence, so this is [unverifiable].
The claim states the reporting car was around [110 km/h](T2) before braking; the GPS speed channel first_value is [113.0](T2) km/h at t=[0.0](T2), which is within ~15% of the claimed [110 km/h](T2).

An emergency braking claim is supported by a rapid speed drop and sustained deceleration: GPS speed dropped from [113.0](T2) to [78.0](T3) km/h between t=[0.0](T3) and t=[3.0](T3), and detect_events reports a sustained accel/decel on accel_x with mean magnitude [0.55](T3) g and peak moving avg [ -0.7](T3) g at t range [0.22–4.11](T3), consistent with strong braking.

The claimant said there was a single impact, but the telemetry shows no high-magnitude impact spike detection: detect_events found no impact-spike CISS detection and accel_x peak_abs_dev is [0.777 g](T2) at t=[1.12](T2) which is below typical impact spike thresholds; GPS shows multiple speed drops but no discrete single impact event, so the assertion of a single impact is [contradicted].

The claimed direction (other car clipped the reporting car while pulling out in front) requires relative-vehicle position or frame-aligned impact axis; device-frame accel axis orientation is undocumented and there is no other-vehicle telemetry, so direction is [unverifiable].

The timeline indicates braking began before the main measured deceleration/peak: detect_events reports braking/sustained decel from t=[0.22](T3) to t=[4.11](T3), while accel_x peak_abs_dev (impact-like peak) occurs at t=[1.12](T2), so emergency braking started before the peak event; GPS speed drop from [113.0](T2) begins at t=[0.0](T3) preceding the accel peak at t=[1.12](T2).

The claim that after the impact the vehicle slowed to walking pace is supported by GPS speed falling to low values later in the window: gps_speed last_value is [42.0](T2) km/h at t=[15.0](T2) and gps_speed minimum is [8.0](T2) km/h in the window; detect_events shows a drop to [10.0](T3) km/h between t=[6.0](T3) and t=[8.0](T3), consistent with slowing to walking pace.

Telemetry shows a modest peak accel deviation of [0.777 g](T2) and integrated delta-V on accel_x of [ -51.8](T2) km/h over the window (device-frame integral), and no high-magnitude impact spike detection; combined these indicate a light/minor collision consistent with a one-time clip rather than a heavy crash.

result.rationale: Sensors confirm high initial speed [113.0 km/h](T2), strong braking activity (sustained accel mean [0.55 g](T3) and speed drops [113.0→78.0 km/h](T3)), and deceleration reaching low speeds ([10.0 km/h](T3) and min [8.0 km/h](T2)). However, there is no clear impact spike or multi-sensor signature to decisively confirm a single contact or its direction, so some narrative points are unverifiable or contradicted by lack of an impact spike.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
